title: What is React's useSyncExternalStore hook?
description: Explain useSyncExternalStore and its use cases.
options: useSyncExternalStore subscribes to external stores (Redux, browser APIs) safely with concurrent rendering. Takes subscribe, getSnapshot, and optional getServerSnapshot. Prevents tearing by ensuring consistent reads during render. Replaced the need for useMutableSource
correct_answer: useSyncExternalStore subscribes to external stores (Redux, browser APIs) safely with concurrent rendering. Takes subscribe, getSnapshot, and optional getServerSnapshot. Prevents tearing by ensuring consistent reads during render. Replaced the need for useMutableSource

title: How does React Server Components streaming work?
description: Explain the streaming mechanism in React Server Components.
options: RSC streaming sends UI as it becomes ready using chunked transfer encoding. Server renders components and streams serialized component tree (RSC payload) to client. Suspense boundaries define streaming units — content inside Suspense streams independently. Client progressively hydrates as chunks arrive
correct_answer: RSC streaming sends UI as it becomes ready using chunked transfer encoding. Server renders components and streams serialized component tree (RSC payload) to client. Suspense boundaries define streaming units — content inside Suspense streams independently. Client progressively hydrates as chunks arrive

title: Implement a React useMediaQuery hook.
description: Create a custom hook that tracks CSS media query matches reactively.

title: What is the React cache() function?
description: Explain React's cache() utility for server components.
options: cache() memoizes async function results per server request. Unlike React.memo (component-level), cache() deduplicates data fetches — calling the same cached function with same args returns the cached promise. Scoped to the server request lifetime, not across requests
correct_answer: cache() memoizes async function results per server request. Unlike React.memo (component-level), cache() deduplicates data fetches — calling the same cached function with same args returns the cached promise. Scoped to the server request lifetime, not across requests

title: How does React's error boundary recovery work with onReset?
options: Error boundaries catch rendering errors via getDerivedStateFromError/componentDidCatch. Libraries like react-error-boundary provide onReset callback and resetKeys: when resetKeys change, the boundary auto-resets; onReset runs cleanup (clear cache, reset state). fallbackRender receives resetErrorBoundary function for retry buttons
correct_answer: Error boundaries catch rendering errors via getDerivedStateFromError/componentDidCatch. Libraries like react-error-boundary provide onReset callback and resetKeys: when resetKeys change, the boundary auto-resets; onReset runs cleanup (clear cache, reset state). fallbackRender receives resetErrorBoundary function for retry buttons

title: Implement a React useThrottle hook.
description: Create a custom hook that throttles a value update.

title: What is React's startViewTransition integration?
description: How does React work with the View Transitions API?
options: React can integrate with the View Transitions API via document.startViewTransition() to animate DOM changes between states. Wrap state updates inside startViewTransition callback; React batches the update, and the browser captures before/after snapshots for CSS-animated transitions. Frameworks like Next.js are adding first-class support
correct_answer: React can integrate with the View Transitions API via document.startViewTransition() to animate DOM changes between states. Wrap state updates inside startViewTransition callback; React batches the update, and the browser captures before/after snapshots for CSS-animated transitions. Frameworks like Next.js are adding first-class support

title: Implement a React useUndoRedo hook.
description: Create a hook that provides undo/redo functionality for state management.

title: What is the difference between React.lazy and dynamic import?
description: Compare React.lazy() with standard dynamic import().
options: React.lazy() wraps dynamic import() to create a lazily-loaded component that works with Suspense. Dynamic import() returns a Promise of the module; React.lazy expects a default export component. React.lazy handles the loading state via Suspense fallback; raw import() requires manual state management for loading/error
correct_answer: React.lazy() wraps dynamic import() to create a lazily-loaded component that works with Suspense. Dynamic import() returns a Promise of the module; React.lazy expects a default export component. React.lazy handles the loading state via Suspense fallback; raw import() requires manual state management for loading/error

title: Implement a React useDebounce hook with cancel support.
description: Create a debounce hook with flush and cancel capabilities.

title: How does React handle hydration errors gracefully?
description: Explain React's hydration error recovery in React 18+.
options: React 18+ detects hydration mismatches and recovers by client-side re-rendering the mismatched subtree. It logs a warning but doesn't crash. For intentional mismatches (timestamps, random IDs), use suppressHydrationWarning. Suspense boundaries can also recover from hydration errors by falling back to client rendering
correct_answer: React 18+ detects hydration mismatches and recovers by client-side re-rendering the mismatched subtree. It logs a warning but doesn't crash. For intentional mismatches (timestamps, random IDs), use suppressHydrationWarning. Suspense boundaries can also recover from hydration errors by falling back to client rendering

title: Implement a React usePreferredLanguage hook.
description: Create a hook that detects the user's preferred browser language.

title: What are React Server Actions?
description: Explain Server Actions in React and how they work.
options: Server Actions are async functions marked with 'use server' that run exclusively on the server. They can be passed to client components as props or used in form action attributes. When invoked, React serializes arguments, sends them to server, executes the function, and returns the result. Enables progressive enhancement — forms work without JavaScript
correct_answer: Server Actions are async functions marked with 'use server' that run exclusively on the server. They can be passed to client components as props or used in form action attributes. When invoked, React serializes arguments, sends them to server, executes the function, and returns the result. Enables progressive enhancement — forms work without JavaScript

title: Implement a React useIdle hook.
description: Create a hook that detects when the user is idle.

title: What is React's taint API for server components?
description: Explain the experimental taintUniqueValue and taintObjectReference APIs.
options: React's taint APIs prevent sensitive data from leaking to client components. taintUniqueValue(message, object, value) marks a specific value (token, key) as non-transferable; taintObjectReference(message, object) marks an entire object. If tainted data is passed to a client component, React throws an error during serialization
correct_answer: React's taint APIs prevent sensitive data from leaking to client components. taintUniqueValue(message, object, value) marks a specific value (token, key) as non-transferable; taintObjectReference(message, object) marks an entire object. If tainted data is passed to a client component, React throws an error during serialization
